title: Add custom initial balance to newly created funds

requirements.text: Add an optional 'Initial Balance' numeric input field to the CreateFundDialog form, allowing users to specify a custom starting amount when creating a new fund.

quotes: i want to be able to add custom amount to my newly created funds

acceptanceCriteria: The CreateFundDialog form includes an optional 'Initial Balance' field (numeric input, defaulting to 0).
The field accepts positive numeric values including decimals.
If left empty or set to 0, fund creation behaves as before with no initial transaction.
The field is clearly labeled and placed logically within the form (e.g., after the fund name).

requirements.text: Update the backend createFund handler (or equivalent fund creation logic in main.mo) to accept an optional initial balance parameter. If provided and greater than zero, automatically record an initial inflow transaction against the newly created fund so the balance reflects the custom amount.

quotes: i want to be able to add custom amount to my newly created funds

acceptanceCriteria: The createFund backend function accepts an optional initialBalance argument (e.g., ?Float or ?Nat).
When initialBalance is provided and > 0, an initial inflow transaction is automatically created for that fund immediately after fund creation.
The fund's balance reflects the initial amount when queried after creation.
Existing fund creation calls with no initial balance continue to work without changes.

requirements.text: Update the useCreateFund mutation hook in useQueries.ts to pass the optional initialBalance value from the dialog form to the backend createFund call.

quotes: i want to be able to add custom amount to my newly created funds

acceptanceCriteria: The useCreateFund mutation accepts and forwards an initialBalance parameter to the backend.
After a successful fund creation with an initial balance, the funds list and balances are re-fetched and reflect the new amount.
A success toast or confirmation is shown after fund creation with the initial balance.

constraints: Do not break existing fund creation flows that omit an initial balance.
Initial balance must be stored as a transaction (inflow) on the fund, not as a raw balance field override, to maintain transaction history integrity.
nonGoals: Editing the initial balance of already-created funds (that is handled by AddFundTransactionDialog).
Adding initial balances to investment or maintenance funds via this flow.
Changing the fund creation UI beyond adding the initial balance field.
